labs.name: External Border Gateway Protocol
labs.one-liner: How to setup eBPG with 3 autonomous systems, using IPv6 & IPv4
labs.purpose: The purpose of this lab is to set up 3 AS’s and use eBGP (external border gateway protocol) to route between the 3 AS’s. To complete this lab, one needs to know how to redistribute routes between gateway protocols and routing protocols, create address-families, and declare eBGP neighbors. And although not central to the eBGP, one still must have the skills needed to set up networks with routing protocols, including advertising interfaces, subnetting, and debugging.

labs.name: Standalone Access Point
labs.one-liner: Configuring an access point that broadcasts multiple SSIDs and leverages RADIUS
labs.purpose: The purpose of this lab is to set up a standalone access point (AP) that broadcasts a 2.4GHz network using a pre-shared key (PSK) to authenticate users; 5GHz network also using a pre-shared key to authenticate users; and a 5GHz network with enterprise authentication using RADIUS. All these Wi-Fi networks will be secured using WPA2.

labs.name: IS-IS Routing Protocol
labs.one-liner: Configuring Intermediate System to Intermediate System across 6 routers
labs.purpose: The purpose of this lab was to set up three Intermediate System to Intermediate System (IS-IS) areas with at least 6 routers in the topology in a linear fashion. This would result in end- to-end connectivity in the host devices at each end of the topology. To complete this task, one needs to learn how to configure IS-IS on a router as well as how to enable IS-IS routing on interfaces.

labs.name: Internal Border Gateway Protocol
labs.one-liner: Configuring iBPG within an autonomous system, using IPv6 & IPv4
labs.purpose: The purpose of this lab is to use Internal Border Gateway Protocol (iBGP) to share routes in and out of an autonomous system (AS) by establishing iBGP neighborships. Additionally, eBGP will be used to share route information between the other two AS’s. As a part of this lab, knowledge about using route-reflectors, declaring iBGP neighbors, and using an interior gateway protocol (IGP) to help BGP packets get navigated between iBGP neighbors will be integral to setting up iBGP. For eBGP, skills about how to redistribute routes and how to use eBGP to route between AS’s will be important for this lab.

labs.name: Layer 2 Attacks
labs.one-liner: Research around performing and preventing common layer 2 attacks
labs.purpose: The purpose of this lab is to perform three layer 2 attacks to understand them. Then to discover how to prevent and mitigate these attacks. This lab’s purpose is to demonstrate practices that can prevent a network from being attacked from layer 2. The following attacks have been performed CAM table overflow, DHCP starvation, VLAN hopping, ARP spoofing

labs.name: Multi-Area OSPF Configuration
labs.one-liner: A "how to" for multi-area OSPF (3 areas; 2 routers in each)
labs.purpose: This lab's purpose is to create a multi-area Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) routing process with at least 3 areas, 2 routers in each area, and one layer 3 switch between 2 areas. For this lab, one needs to understand how to create subnets for networks, advertise interfaces for OSPF, and connect links between devices.
